steps.description: Add TTL-related constants and structures to lmdb.h and mdb.c. Define MDB_TTL_EXPIRED error code, MDB_TTL flag for put operations, and internal structures for storing expiration timestamps.

lmdb.h: Add #define MDB_TTL 0x1000 to flags for mdb_put_ttl
Add #define MDB_TTL_EXPIRED (-30800) as new error code
Add function prototype: int mdb_put_ttl(MDB_txn *txn, MDB_dbi dbi, MDB_val *key, MDB_val *data, time_t expiry);
Add function prototype: int mdb_purge_expired(MDB_txn *txn, MDB_dbi dbi);

steps.description: Modify mdb_put to support TTL by adding a wrapper function mdb_put_ttl that encodes expiry timestamp into value data (prepend 8-byte timestamp) and calls mdb_put with MDB_TTL flag. Update internal value handling to store expiry separately in a TTL sub-database.

Modify mdb_get to check for expiration before returning data. Decode expiry timestamp from stored value (first 8 bytes) or query TTL sub-database, compare with current time, and return MDB_TTL_EXPIRED if expired. Ensure backward compatibility for non-TTL data.

Implement mdb_purge_expired function that scans the TTL sub-database (or main database with TTL data) and deletes all expired key-value pairs using cursor operations. Use mdb_cursor_get to iterate and mdb_cursor_del to remove expired entries.

Update internal cursor operations (mdb_cursor_put, mdb_cursor_del) to handle TTL data correctly. Ensure that when inserting with TTL, the expiry is stored in the TTL sub-database, and when deleting, both main and TTL entries are removed.
Add helper functions for TTL management: encode expiry into value, decode expiry from value, and check if a key is expired. Integrate these helpers into mdb_get and mdb_put_ttl.
Modify transaction commit and abort logic to ensure TTL sub-database is properly handled. Update mdb_txn_commit and mdb_txn_abort to clean up TTL-related resources if needed.
Update database open/close functions (mdb_dbi_open, mdb_dbi_close) to initialize and manage the TTL sub-database. Ensure the TTL sub-database is created automatically when TTL is used.
Add error handling and validation for TTL operations. Ensure that invalid expiry times (e.g., past times) are rejected, and that MDB_TTL_EXPIRED is returned appropriately in mdb_get.

Test the implementation by adding simple test cases in existing test files or creating a new test program. Verify that mdb_put_ttl stores expiry, mdb_get returns MDB_TTL_EXPIRED for expired keys, and mdb_purge_expired removes expired data.
